description: Minimal spec for autopilot-selftest — exists to dogfood check_spec_constraints.py end-to-end against a real consumer repo. Keep it small; this is not where autopilot's own invariants live.

selftest_shape.description: The consumer caller workflow consumes autopilot via a cross-repo uses: ref, never a local path.
goals: Preserve the physical boundary between implementation (autopilot) and consumer (autopilot-selftest) so the separation cannot be blurred by accident.

no_local_uses_paths.description: Negative: no workflow in this repo may reference autopilot via a local `./` path. Only cross-repo `<owner>/autopilot/...@<ref>` is permitted. Enforces the physical boundary between consumer and implementation.

uses_marketplace_workflow.cmd: for f in $PROJECT_ROOT/.github/workflows/*.yml; do grep -qE 'uses:[[:space:]]*Clockwork-Pilot/autopilot/\.github/workflows/[a-z0-9_-]+\.yml@' "$f" || { echo "$f does not reference a Clockwork-Pilot/autopilot marketplace workflow via uses:"; exit 1; }; done
uses_marketplace_workflow.description: Positive: every workflow file in this repo must contain at least one `uses: Clockwork-Pilot/autopilot/.github/workflows/<name>.yml@<ref>` line. Proves autopilot-selftest is actively consuming the marketplace surface, not just structurally avoiding local paths.

autopilot_submodule_healthy.description: Autopilot submodule is checked out and its own constraint suite passes.
goals: Dogfood autopilot from the consumer side: if autopilot ships broken invariants, the selftest should refuse to pass.

autopilot_constraints_pass.description: Behavioral: autopilot's own spec constraints must pass. Runs inside the autopilot-ws docker image via the same entrypoint CI uses, so a regression in autopilot (or a broken submodule pin) fails the selftest.
